records.display_name: Phase II corridors implemented and planned

payload.claim_text: NYCDOT and NYCT identified 16 corridors for future BRT development in Phase II. Since completion of Phase II study, two Phase II corridors have been implemented: Webster Avenue and Jackson Heights to LaGuardia Airport, with 125th Street/Astoria Boulevard route scheduled for Spring 2014. Planning is underway for improvements along Woodhaven Boulevard and Utica Avenue.
payload.subject: Phase II BRT corridors

payload.claim_text: Six new SBS services were implemented since 2008 (along with M60 SBS coming in Spring 2014). The chief goal of the SBS program is to make buses faster, more reliable and easily identified as a premium service. SBS serves all five boroughs, focusing on high-ridership bus routes. SBS routes are complementary to the city's subway system. Each SBS project has included extensive public engagement involving community boards, business improvement districts, merchant associations, civic groups and officials. Dedicated bus lanes, off-board fare payment, transit signal priority and branding are the key elements. NYCDOT and NYCT focused on fast implementation within constrained rights-of-way. These projects have led to up to 20% faster bus service and 10-20% increases in corridor ridership in the first year.
payload.subject: SBS program

payload.claim_text: Bx41 SBS was planned and implemented more quickly than any other SBS project to date.
payload.subject: Bx41 SBS

payload.claim_text: M15 SBS is the highest ridership bus route in NYC.
payload.subject: M15 SBS

payload.claim_text: Ridership on M34 SBS has grown even as ridership on other Manhattan crosstown routes has declined.
payload.subject: M34 SBS

payload.claim_text: SBS elements include: Off-board fare payment, Low-floor three-door buses with speed boarding, On-street bus lanes, Transit signal priority, Wider stop spacing, Improved passenger information
payload.description: Key bus priority elements implemented across SBS routes
payload.subject: SBS program
